results.title: AES Just Posted a $30/hr Intern JD That Kills the Leetcode Screen · Refolk
highlights:
The post comes from AES (associatedenvironmentalsystems.com). It pays $30 to $40 per hour for a remote summer intern, June 15 through August 22. Applications closed June 8. The team is a senior lead, a part-time backend engineer, and the intern. The stack is Next.js 14 with App Router and RSC, TypeScript strict, Prisma on Postgres, Tailwind with shadcn/ui, NextAuth, Twilio, and the Anthropic SDK. None of that is what made the post travel.
...
> The bar is NOT raw coding ability. We use Claude Code for all code generation. The bar is the ability to direct an AI at production engineering and catch it when it's wrong.
...
Then, in the same post: "No leetcode. No whiteboard. Show us your best AI-built work, a PR, a deployed app, or a repo." Must-haves include a CLAUDE.md in the submitted work, one shipped end-to-end project a stranger can use, and what the JD calls "schema/data-flow literacy."
...
AES names three things it actually screens for: schema literacy, diff reading, and end-to-end ownership. None of them require typing code from a blank file. All three are measurable in a take-home that takes under three hours.
...
Diff reading. Paste a 300 to 600 line PR generated by Claude Code or Cursor against a real repo. Ask the candidate to leave review comments. The signal is not whether they catch the obvious bug. It is whether they catch the "almost right" issues: the silently-swallowed error, the off-by-one on a paginated query, the auth check that runs after the data fetch. This is the same skill 66% of developers in the Infobip survey said is their biggest frustration with AI output, that "AI solutions that are almost right, but not quite." You are literally hiring for the gap.
...
Put those numbers next to the 26.9% of production code that is now AI-authored and you have the JD's logic in one line: the bottleneck role is the human who catches the wrongness. AES is not replacing the intern with Claude. AES is using Claude to make a one-intern team viable that previously was not. That is the contrarian read on what AI is doing to entry-level hiring at small companies. It is enabling it, not killing it, but only for candidates who can do the triad.
...
The AES JD names Claude Code. Cora AI names Cursor and Claude Code. Be specific. The DX-cited stat that the best 2026 developers use 2.3 AI tools on average (typically an AI IDE plus a terminal agent) tells you the shape: ask for fluency with one IDE-integrated tool and one agentic CLI. Vague "experience with AI tools" gets you vague candidates.
...
This is the move I expect to spread fastest. A CLAUDE.md (or AGENTS.md, the GitHub convention) is the candidate's documented theory of how to direct an agent at their specific codebase. It reveals context-engineering taste in a way no resume bullet can. Asking for one as part of the application is the cleanest screening artifact I have seen in a decade.
...
June 2026 HN thread has founding-engineer roles at $190 to $250k (Cora AI) and $105 to $125k CAD (Deep Core Technology) using identical framing. A separate founding role in the thread says the company generates 90%-plus of code with AI and uses a short paid work trial instead of algorithm rounds. The pattern is consistent from intern through founding. The constraint is not seniority. It is whether your product depends on raw algorithmic depth.
...
they think about
...
The Forrester read was that AI would gut entry-level hiring. The AES JD suggests the opposite at small teams. AI is what makes a one-intern team viable. The catch is that the intern has to clear the new triad (schema literacy, diff reading, end-to-end ownership) on day one, because there is no senior engineer with capacity to teach those skills from scratch. So entry-level hiring survives, but it gets selective in a different way: less credentialed, more demonstrated, and faster to evaluate when the artifacts are public.

highlights:
# Senior Structural Engineer
...
Are you ready to be part of a company that's not just talking about the future, but actively shaping it? Join The AES Corporation (NYSE: AES), a Fortune 500 company that's leading the charge in the global energy revolution. With operations spanning 14 countries, AES is committed to shaping a future through innovation and collaboration. Our dedication to innovation has earned us recognition as one of the Top Ten Best Workplaces for Innovators by Fast Company in 2022. And with our certification as a Great Place to Work, you can be confident that you're joining a company that values its people just as much as its groundbreaking ideas.
...
AES Clean Energy is seeking a highly motivated and experienced Senior Structural Engineer to support design development, plans review, and construction of wind, photovoltaic (PV) and grid-scale battery storage systems in coordination with the AES CE Business Development, Engineering and Construction teams.
...
The Senior Structural Engineer will be responsible for providing professional and technical support to multiple wind, solar PV and battery storage projects. Design review of structural plans for AES CE Engineering, construction alternatives and structural solutions, and quality control and assurance reviews will also be a part of their daily routine.
...
The Senior Structural Engineer supports a larger development and engineering project team and serves as the subject matter expert for technical and engineering matters related to structural engineering design. This position requires the candidate to have an extensive knowledge of pile load testing, Lpile reporting and analysis, shallow and deep foundations, concrete and structural steel design, corrosion of steel and concrete, and wind turbine generator (WTG) foundation design including spread footings and P&H tensionless pier. Excellent communication, task management and planning skills are needed, in addition to technical breadth.
...
The successful candidate will have a depth of experience in design, engineering, and analysis of civil plans, structural piles, and structural foundations for large scale renewable energy development projects. Experience with wind, PV systems and battery energy storage systems (BESS) ranging from 500 kW to 200 MW is preferable.
...
While Senior Structural Civil engineering is the primary qualification, the role will require the review of information related to general structural elements of photovoltaic system, wind, and battery storage design. The successful applicant, while not an expert, will be comfortable with high-level review of all elements of practical wind, BESS and PV system design.

We are seeking a highly skilled AI/ML Engineer to develop advanced AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems.

Python AWS Azure GCP Databricks LangChain LangGraph LlamaIndex Hugging Face AutoGen

This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities

...

...

...

...

...
- Solid understanding of ML system design, MLOps principles, and end-to-end model lifecycle management.
...
- Experience with distributed computing frameworks and relational/non-relational databases.
...
- Familiarity with Databricks and large-scale data processing environments.
...
- Experience working with generative AI frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Hugging Face, or AutoGen.
...
- Exposure to agentic AI systems, AI orchestration, or LLM-based decision workflows is highly desirable.
...

The Civil Engineer will be responsible for providing support to the Civil Engineering team and guidance to Strat Dev, RE and Dev while assisting the Dev Eng and Exec Eng group with early-through late-stage civil design plans. This role will include collaboration with Permitting, Geotech and Civil subject matter experts (SME), review of Hydrology Studies, stormwater design development, supporting entitlement acquisition, and providing Pre-construction support. As projects move from Dev Eng into Exec Eng, the Civil Engineer will support Pre-Construction, Exec Eng, Engineering Construction, Owners Eng and Procurement (EPC) detailed civil design reviews and provide Civil Engineering recommendations. Periodic support during the construction phase, collaboration with Compliance on stormwater management, support of requests for information and project close out support for wind, solar PV and BESS projects will be expected.

The Civil Engineer supports the Civil Engineering team and a larger development and execution project team and serves in support of the Subject Matter Experts (SME) for technical and engineering matters related to civil engineering design, stormwater management, and construction recommendations. This candidate will work closely with other Civil Engineering Team members and assist both Dev Eng and Exec Eng in providing oversight on conceptual through IFC designs by others. Design plan reviews may be performed, optimized, and reviewed utilizing AutoCAD, Civil 3D and Revu Bluebeam. This position requires the candidate to have exposure and knowledge of the regulatory, permitting, and construction process for land development projects. Excellent communication, task management and planning skills needed, in addition to the requirement for breadth in technical knowledge.

results.title: Data Scientist, Mid at Booz Allen Hamilton | Jeremy
highlights:
Data Scientist, Mid at Bo
...
Data Scientist, Mid at Booz Allen Hamilton | Jeremy
...
[Booz Allen Hamiltonboozallen.com](https://tryjeremy.com/companies/booz-allen-hamilton)# Data Scientist, Mid
...
McLean, VAWashington, DCOn-siteMid-LevelSeniorML / AIData ScienceClearance Required
...
Mid-level data scientist role at a defense contractor focused on turning complex intelligence and operational data into actionable insights using machine learning and advanced analytics. Requires active TS/SCI clearance.
...
+ benefits · Within US market range for ML / AI (Mid)
...
As a data scientist, you’re excited at the prospect of unlocking the secrets held by a data set, and you’re fascinated by the possibilities presented by IoT, machine learning, and artificial intelligence. In an increasingly connected world, massive amounts of structured and unstructured data open new opportunities. As a data scientist at Booz Allen, you can help turn these complex data sets into useful information to solve global challenges. Across private and public sectors—from fraud detection to cancer research to national intelligence—we need you to help find the answers in the data. 
On our team, you’ll use your leadership skills and data science expertise to create real-world impact. You’ll work closely with clients to understand their questions and needs and then dig into their data-rich environments to find the pieces of their information puzzle. You’ll guide teammates and lead the development of algorithms and systems. You’ll use the right combination of tools and frameworks to turn sets of disparate data points into objective answers to advise your clients as they make informed decisions. Ultimately, you’ll provide a deep understanding of the data, what it all means, and how it can be used.
...
* 5+ years of experience using data science programming languages such as Python or R
...
* Experience creating ETL pipelines, including querying APIs, cleaning and conditioning data, and creating schemas for data storage
* Experience with version control tools, including git, GitLab, GitHub, or Bitbucket
* Knowledge of statistical analysis, predictive modeling, and machine learning methods
* Ability to design and develop automated analytic software, techniques, and algorithms
* Ability to develop solutions and integrate and extend free and open-source software (FOSS) or COTS products
...
* Ability to generate data visualizations with tools such as Kibana, Tableau, or Power BI
...
* Active TS/SCI clearance; willingness to take a polygraph exam
...
* Experience working with GEOINT, HUMINT, MASINT, OSINT, and SIGINT and the data and tools associated with these disciplines
* Experience with intelligence or operations analysis in the DoD or Intelligence Community
* Experience developing and implementing ontological frameworks
* Experience in the development of algorithms leveraging R, Python, or SQL and NoSQL 
* Experience working with Deep Learning and AI frameworks including PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Keras
* Experience working with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and Large Language Models (LLM)
* Experience with distributed data and computing tools, including MapReduce, Hadoop, Hive, EMR, Kafka, Spark, Gurobi, or MySQL 
* Experience with visualization packages, including Plotly, Seaborn, or ggplot2 
* Knowledge of DevOps, including CI/CD pipelines and Jenkins
...

**Candidate AI Usage Policy**
...
AI is a part of our daily work at Booz Allen, and we are committed to the responsible and ethical use of AI tools. However, we want to ensure a fair candidate process based on **your **own skills and knowledge. As part of this commitment, the use of artificial intelligence (AI) or other tools to assist with responses during interviews (whether in-person or virtual) is prohibited **unless permission is explicitly provided**.

# Experienced Data Scientist – AI/Machine Learning Developer – Generative AI Platform Development
...
Join arenaflex, and you'll be part of a team that's changing the world one innovation at a time. We are currently seeking a talented and passionate Data Scientist to join our Cloud Technologies Application Platform team and help us build the future of developer experience through cutting-edge artificial intelligence solutions.
...
Are you ready to take your career to the next level with a company that values innovation and excellence? arenaflex is looking for an experienced Data Scientist to join our dynamic team as an AI/Machine Learning Developer. In this role, you will be instrumental in designing and executing our artificial intelligence strategy to elevate our developer experience platform to unprecedented heights.
...
This is a unique opportunity to work at the forefront of generative AI technology, collaborating with multidisciplinary teams to develop innovative solutions that will transform how developers create applications at arenaflex. You'll leverage your expertise in machine learning, deep learning, and large language models to solve complex business challenges and drive measurable impact across the organization.
...
As a Data Scientist at arenaflex, you will be responsible for driving our AI initiatives and contributing to the development of next-generation developer tools. Your key responsibilities will include:
...
Design, develop, and implement machine learning models and algorithms that enhance our developer experience platform, ensuring alignment with arenaflex's overall technology strategy.
...
Build, train, and fine-tune machine learning models, including foundation models and large language models, to address various business problems and improve developer productivity.

values, identifying outliers

...
Lead model deployment for experimentation, implementing A/B testing, monitoring model performance, and iterating on solutions based on empirical results.
...
Work closely with AI engineers, software developers, product managers, and other data scientists to deliver end-to-end AI-enabled solutions.
...
Experience with training models or utilizing foundation models and fine-tuning them to meet custom business requirements.

Minimum of 5+ years of progressive experience in the software industry with a focused specialization in machine learning and artificial intelligence.

...
- Advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in Data Science, Computer Science, or related field

- Experience applying machine learning techniques specifically in developer productivity or software development lifecycle contexts

- Knowledge of cloud platforms and infrastructure (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud)

- Familiarity with MLOps practices and deployment pipelines

- Experience with prompt engineering and LLM optimization techniques

If you are passionate about artificial intelligence, machine learning, and want to work on projects that will shape the future of technology, arenaflex is the place for you. We are looking for individuals who bring enthusiasm, dedication, and fresh perspectives to their work – people who are ready to push boundaries and achieve extraordinary results.
